channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for a lawsuit over COVID-19 pandemic-era unemployment claims in Alabama
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — People left waiting for months on their unemployment claims during the coronavirus pandemic in Alabama can sue the state, the U.S. Supreme Court said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Colorado avalanche victim rescued in dramatic video
item.description: A Colorado avalanche victim was rescued in Vail Pass after spending more than an hour underneath the snow, the Summit County Sheriff’s Office said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Their mosque burned down in LA-area wildfire. They're still determined to gather for Ramadan
item.description: Members of Masjid Al-Taqwa, a mosque which burned down in one of the recent Los Angeles-area wildfires, are scrambling to find a space where they can gather, pray and support one another during the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: What to know about Fort Knox's gold depository
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump says Elon Musk will be looking at Fort Knox, the legendary depository in Kentucky for American gold reserves, to make sure the gold is still there.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA REYNOLDS The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CA gas prices highest in nation, Bay Area averaging $5-plus per gallon
item.description: (KRON) -- California gas prices are known to be significantly higher compared to other states in the country. On Thursday, the American Automobile Association (AAA) announced California gas is officially the priciest in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers beginning this week likely mean the end of the agency's plan to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections, experts say.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It was obvious to Christine Smith Olsey that her son was not doing well at school, despite educators telling her to leave it to the experts. The second-grade student stumbled over words, and other kids teased him so much he started to call himself “an idiot.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Smithfield fined $10K after worker locked in oven
item.description: "An employee performing work inside Smokehouse #18 became entrapped after the doors were closed and sealed," reads the citation.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Newton, NewsNation

item.title: Lifetime, Chili’s celebrate National Margarita Day with new rom-com feature
item.description: Love, laughter, and lime wedges take center stage in Lifetime’s latest feel-good romance, created in partnership with Chili’s Bar & Grill, to celebrate National Margarita Day, which is coming up on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman injured after ice slides off roof of tractor-trailer
item.description: Dan DeCesare said his wife, who was in the passenger seat, was hit directly in the face and chest by the chunk of ice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: David Boren, a former Oklahoma governor and veteran US senator and university president, dies at 83
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Former Oklahoma Gov. David Boren, who became one of the nation's youngest governors in the 1970s at age 33 and later helped shape national intelligence as a U.S. senator, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: TIM TALLEY and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration nixes legal representation for thousands of migrant children
item.description: President Trump has made cracking down on immigration a key tenet of his second term, including in some cases the elimination of certain government services.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Florida leaders warn deadly consequences of ‘senior assassin’ game
item.description: They are urging parents and guardians to discuss with their children the potential dangers of playing this game.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, NewsNation

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, KRON4

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Police hunt for ‘male companion’ seen fleeing slain college student’s apartment
item.description: Police are searching for a person of interest in the death of Menghan Zhuang after a man was seen climbing from her apartment window shortly before she was found unresponsive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most worried DOGE cuts could hurt services in their communities: Survey
item.description: Most U.S. adults in the latest survey, 62 percent, disagreed with the notion that the president is authorized to terminate federal employees who do not align with his views or agenda.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black and blue, or white and gold? 'The dress' turns 10 years old
item.description: Ten years ago today, a debate started when a dress was posted on the internet asking what color people were seeing.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reputed migrant gang members busted in NYC drug, gun raid but likely to avoid prosecution
item.description: Two suspected members of the violent Tren de Aragua (TdA) gang were busted in a gun and drug raid in a Big Apple auto repair shop earlier this month – and have since been cut loose on lesser charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agriculture Department tries to rehire fired workers tied to bird flu response
item.description: The Agriculture Department is scrambling to rehire several workers who were involved in the government's response to the ongoing bird flu outbreak that has devastated egg and poultry farms over the past three years.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: RFK Jr. eyes childhood vaccine schedule review, says 'nothing off limits' in HHS meeting
item.description: Kennedy urged his new staff to drop any preconceived notions of who he is, but also said he wouldn’t stop asking “difficult questions” that have led to “a lot of unpopular conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Brutal polar vortex cold shutters Midwest schools, breaks North Dakota record
item.description: More than 95 million people are facing gripping cold Tuesday as a polar vortex sends temperatures plunging to record levels, closing schools, bursting pipes and forcing communities to set up more temporary shelters for the homeless.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
